baseline.$description: Baseline breakpoint. The smallest viewport the system targets. Layouts should be functional at this width.

mobile.$description: Mobile breakpoint. Use as the boundary between baseline and mobile-class layouts.

tablet.$description: Tablet breakpoint. Use as the boundary at which tablet-class layouts engage.

notebook.$description: Notebook breakpoint. Use as the boundary at which compact-laptop-class layouts engage.

laptop.$description: Laptop breakpoint. Use as the boundary at which standard-laptop-class layouts engage.

desktop.$description: Desktop breakpoint. Use as the boundary at which desktop-class layouts engage.

widescreen.$description: Widescreen breakpoint. Use as the boundary at which widescreen-class layouts engage.
